text: India has the world's 4th largest military. Three branches: Indian Army (active: 12.37 lakh), Indian Navy (active: 67,228), Indian Air Force (active: 1.4 lakh). Paramilitary forces: CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, NSG, Assam Rifles (combined: 10+ lakh). Supreme Commander: President of India. Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) position created in 2019.

India's defence budget for 2024-25 is Rs 6.21 lakh crore (about $74.5 billion), approximately 1.9% of GDP. Breakdown: Army 56%, Air Force 23%, Navy 15%, Defence R&D 6%. Capital expenditure (new equipment): Rs 1.72 lakh crore. Revenue expenditure (salaries, maintenance): Rs 2.82 lakh crore. India is the world's 4th largest defence spender.

Defence indigenization push: Two positive indigenization lists banning import of 400+ items. Defence corridors in UP (Lucknow-Kanpur-Agra) and Tamil Nadu (Chennai-Coimbatore-Salem). LCA Tejas fighter jet, INS Vikrant aircraft carrier, BrahMos missile, Arjun MBT, and Pinaka MLRS are key indigenous platforms. Defence exports rose from Rs 1,500 crore (2017) to Rs 21,000 crore (2024).

India's nuclear doctrine follows a 'No First Use' (NFU) policy with 'Credible Minimum Deterrence'. Nuclear triad: Land-based Agni missiles (Agni-V range: 5,000+ km), Sea-based SSBN submarines (INS Arihant), Air-based fighter jets. India conducted nuclear tests in 1974 (Smiling Buddha) and 1998 (Pokhran-II) under PM Vajpayee. India is not a signatory to the NPT but maintains voluntary moratorium on testing.
